description: Production task (Media workflow-start): write the opening hook (first line / first 5 seconds) for a short-form video. Hooks have huge quality variance — generic/cliché vs a sharp curiosity-gap — so the strict judge discriminates hard. Tasks include hard constraints (no question form, exact length, banned clichés), a calibration/tone trap (a serious topic where clickbait is wrong), a platform-fit task, and a rewrite-a-weak-hook task. Deterministic checks anchor the objective constraints (length, banned words, format); the judge grades hook craft: curiosity/tension, specificity, honesty (no empty clickbait), and constraint adherence.

tasks.title: One-line hook with a real curiosity gap
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=15 words) for a 60-second video titled "Why your savings account is quietly losing you money." It must open a curiosity/stakes gap that makes a saver keep watching — concrete, not a generic intro. Return ONLY the hook line, nothing else.

criteria.name: hook_craft
criteria.guidance: Opens a genuine curiosity or stakes gap a saver feels personally (e.g. names the hidden loss, contradicts a comfortable assumption), specific and punchy. Penalize generic openers ('Did you know savings accounts...'), restating the title, vague 'let's talk about money', or anything that doesn't create a reason to keep watching.

criteria.name: concision
criteria.guidance: Tight, <=15 words, one line, no preamble.

tasks.title: Hook that must NOT be a question (constraint trap)
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=14 words) for a video "3 budgeting mistakes that keep you broke." Hard constraint: the hook must be a STATEMENT, not a question (no '?'). Cheap hooks default to a question — resist it. Return ONLY the hook line.

criteria.name: statement_hook
criteria.guidance: A compelling declarative hook (a bold claim, a stakes statement, a counterintuitive fact) — NOT a question, and not a question reworded with a period. It should still create tension/curiosity. Penalize any interrogative phrasing, weak generic statements, or restating the title.

tasks.title: Hook avoiding banned clichés
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=16 words) for a video "How I learned Spanish in 4 months." Hard constraint: do NOT use any of these cliché openers: "In this video", "Did you know", "Let's dive in", "Have you ever", "Today I'm going to". Return ONLY the hook line.

deterministic.pattern: in this video|did you know|let'?s dive in|have you ever|today i'?m going to

criteria.name: freshness
criteria.guidance: A fresh, specific hook that earns attention (a surprising method, a concrete result, a contrarian take) without any banned cliché or a thin paraphrase of one. Penalize formulaic openers, vagueness, or restating the title.

tasks.title: Calibration trap: serious topic, no exploitative clickbait
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=18 words) for a video "Talking to your parents about their end-of-life wishes." It must be compelling enough to retain viewers BUT respectful and non-exploitative — no fear-baiting or sensational clickbait on a grief-adjacent topic. Return ONLY the hook line.

criteria.name: tone_calibration
criteria.guidance: Draws the viewer in through genuine relevance/empathy (the hard-but-important conversation, the cost of avoiding it) while staying respectful. HEAVILY penalize exploitative fear-bait, sensationalism, or morbid clickbait ('This will happen to your parents...'), and also penalize a hook so bland it gives no reason to watch. This tests whether the model calibrates tone to a sensitive topic.

criteria.name: compelling
criteria.guidance: Still creates a real reason to keep watching, not a flat PSA.

tasks.title: Platform-fit: a LinkedIn hook (not TikTok energy)
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=20 words) for a LinkedIn video aimed at B2B founders: "The hiring mistake that cost us six months." Match LinkedIn register — credible, professional, insight-led — NOT TikTok hype or emoji-bait. Return ONLY the hook line.

criteria.name: platform_register
criteria.guidance: Reads as a credible, insight-led LinkedIn hook for founders (a specific costly lesson, a concrete claim, professional voice that promises a takeaway). Penalize TikTok-style hype, emoji-bait, vague motivational fluff, or a register mismatch. Should still be a genuine hook, not a dry title.

tasks.title: Exact structure: 2-line hook (tension then promise)
tasks.prompt: Write a TWO-LINE hook for a video "I quit caffeine for 30 days." Exact structure: line 1 sets the tension/stakes, line 2 delivers the promise of what they'll learn. Two lines only, each <=12 words. Return ONLY the two lines separated by a newline.

criteria.name: structure_and_craft
criteria.guidance: Exactly two lines: line 1 establishes tension/stakes, line 2 the payoff/promise — both tight (<=12 words). The two lines should work together as an escalating hook. Penalize a single line, three+ lines, both lines doing the same job, or generic/weak content.

tasks.title: Specificity beats vague hype
tasks.prompt: Write the opening hook (one line, <=15 words) for a video "How a small Etsy shop hit $10k/month." Use the concrete specifics (small shop, $10k/month) to create the hook — avoid vague hype like 'this changed everything' or 'you won't believe'. Return ONLY the hook line.

deterministic.pattern: you won'?t believe|this changed everything|mind-?blowing|secret to success

criteria.name: specificity
criteria.guidance: Leads with the concrete, credible specifics (a small/solo shop reaching $10k/month, the surprising lever) to create the hook. Penalize vague hype, empty superlatives, clickbait that doesn't deliver, or dropping the concrete numbers that make it believable and intriguing.

tasks.title: Rewrite a weak hook into a strong one
tasks.prompt: This hook is weak: "In this video, I'm going to talk about some tips for saving money on groceries." Rewrite it as a single strong hook line (<=15 words) that creates curiosity and is specific — for the same grocery-savings video. Return ONLY the rewritten hook line.

deterministic.pattern: in this video|going to talk about|some tips

criteria.name: improvement
criteria.guidance: A clearly stronger hook than the original: specific, curiosity-creating, removes the throat-clearing ('in this video', 'going to talk about', 'some tips'). It should name a concrete angle (a surprising amount saved, a counterintuitive method). Penalize a rewrite that's still generic, keeps the filler, or doesn't materially improve on the weak original.
